title: Single-section regenerate persists by merging into the LAST STORED draft, silently dropping unsaved edits to other sections
expected: Per-section regenerate preserves the rest of the letter AND my edits — the version it saves should reflect every section as it currently stands, including textarea edits I made to other sections.
got: On a single-section regenerate, the server merges the new section into getLatestDraft (the last STORED version), not into the sections the client currently holds. Plain textarea edits are local-only (editBody updates state, no save; there is no autosave and no standalone Save button). So if I edit Section A, then regenerate Section B, the persisted version merges new-B into a stored draft that never had my A edit. The client UI keeps A for the session, but on reopen the case hydrates initialSections from getLatestDraft — my A edit is gone. Silent loss on a paid path.
suggested_acceptance: Either (a) send the client's full current sections with the focus regenerate and merge server-side against THOSE, or (b) auto-persist textarea edits (debounced no-charge save) before a regenerate, or (c) add an explicit Save edits button and block regenerate on unsaved edits. The persisted version must never silently lose a section edit.
l2_priority: Live: edit section A, regenerate section B, reload the case — assert section A's edit survives in the hydrated draft (currently predicted to be lost).

title: Per-section regenerate prompt does not receive the rest of the letter, so it can't keep narrative continuity with the other sections
expected: When I regenerate one section, the model should see the surrounding letter (at least the intro/adjacent sections) so the regenerated prose doesn't repeat or contradict the rest.
got: buildSectionPrompt is fed ONLY the focus criterion's data (name/status/evidence/rationale + that criterion's exhibits) — not the other sections' bodies. The regenerated section is coherent with its own criterion and cites the right exhibits, but cannot reference or stay consistent with the rest of the letter. The persistence layer DOES preserve the other sections (merge-by-heading), so this is a prose-continuity gap, not structural loss.
suggested_acceptance: Optionally include the headings (and trimmed bodies) of the other sections in the section prompt as read-only context, so the regenerated section stays continuous with the letter.
l2_priority: Live: judge whether a regenerated section reads as part of one letter or as a standalone blurb that repeats the intro.

title: RFE response generation has NO live adjudication gate (drafts do) — no fabrication/case-law/wrong-code scan on RFE output
expected: An RFE response is paid, attorney-signed work product on a 60-90 day clock; it should get the same live adjudication-risk scan (fabricated specifics, case-law flag, wrong visa code) that the draft path attaches.
got: The /api/draft spec wires an adjudicate hook (runAdjudication over draft output); the /api/rfe spec has no adjudicate field, so RFE responses carry the DISCLAIMER but get no AdjudicationReport. A hallucinated number or a leaked case-law cite in an RFE response is not surfaced to me the way it is in a draft.
suggested_acceptance: Add an adjudicate hook to the RFE spec (operation 'rfe' already has gates: classification/fabrication/caselaw in runAdjudication) so RFE output gets the same risk badge as a draft.
l2_priority: Live: generate an RFE response with a number not in the record and confirm whether anything flags it (predicted: nothing does).

title: RFE prompt grounds on the scored criteria + evidence + exhibits, but NOT on the actual drafted petition-letter prose
expected: A point-by-point RFE response should reference the original petition. Ideally that includes the letter I actually filed, not only the criteria structure.
got: buildRfePrompt receives the petition CRITERIA (name/status/evidence/rationale) + the fused vault exhibits + the RFE notice text — a real, grounded crosswalk to the criterion at issue. It does NOT receive getLatestDraft's letter sections. For most RFEs the criteria+evidence is the right grounding (it IS the petition's argument), but a senior crosswalk to 'the original petition' would also want the filed prose. Net: structurally strong, one rung short of full fidelity.
suggested_acceptance: Optionally fuse the latest stored draft sections into the RFE prompt as the 'as-filed petition' context, so the response can quote/track the letter's own language.
l2_priority: Live: confirm the RFE response actually names the petition's specific evidence + criterion (it should, from the criteria grounding) and judge whether missing the letter prose hurts.

title: RfeStudio is correctly gated to Filed cases only (reachability confirmed)
expected: Per the journey, the RFE studio should appear only on a Filed case.
got: CaseDetailView renders RfeStudio only when status === 'Filed'. The /api/rfe DB path additionally resolves the case owner-or-configured-attorney before any charge. Reachability for Devin (operating as owner or demo-unlocked attorney) is sound.
suggested_acceptance: Keep the Filed-only gate.
l2_priority: n-a

title: Exhibit numbering is monotonic and never-reused; misfits go to Unsorted, not a fabricated bucket
expected: Monotonic exhibit index that stays sane across refile/remove; no silent wrong-bucketing.
got: addCaseDocument assigns Ex. N from a transactional high-water mark (greatest(doc_seq, max(ord)) + 1), so deleting the top exhibit never frees its number for reuse and refile keeps the exhibit with its document. coerceBucket forces any out-of-pack label to 'Unsorted'; the live evidence-bucket adjudication gate fails a bucket not in the pack. The gap read (summarizeVault) is derived from real per-criterion coverage. The categorize prompt receives the real document name + content and the actual classification's criteria.
suggested_acceptance: Do not touch the high-water-mark exhibit assignment or the Unsorted coercion.
l2_priority: Live: confirm the model buckets a real award/press/publication into the CORRECT criterion (not just a valid one) — bucketing accuracy is the L2 quality question.

title: Refile to a wrong bucket is unvalidated and silently allowed; the gap read can then misreport coverage
expected: If I refile a document to a criterion it doesn't support, the tool should at least not present that as a real coverage win — and ideally warn.
got: onRefile just changes the document's criterion via a server action with no re-categorization or sanity check; summarizeVault then counts the (possibly wrong) bucket as covering that criterion and clears the gap badge. This is a manual-override convenience, but a mis-refile silently inflates coverage, which is exactly the kind of false 'criterion covered' read Devin distrusts.
suggested_acceptance: Refile is a legitimate manual override; acceptable as-is for an MVP. If hardened, flag a refiled doc whose facts don't match the new bucket, or keep coverage as 'documents present' rather than 'criterion proven'.
l2_priority: n-a

title: Roadmap derives from real case state; real cases list above the mock demo and deep-link correctly
expected: Dashboard shows my real cases above any mock; case detail shows an accurate stage stepper and a next action.
got: caseRoadmap(status, {hasEvidence, hasDraft}) computes done/current/upcoming from real status + real evidence/draft flags. The dashboard renders YourCasesCard (real, DB-backed) above the illustrative mock masthead; empty state CTAs to /qualify; each case deep-links to /dashboard/cases/[id], hydrated from stored criteria/draft/rfe/documents/events behind the IDOR-closed resolveCase gate.
suggested_acceptance: Keep the real-cases-above-mock ordering and the state-derived stepper.
l2_priority: n-a

title: A fixed mock 'Dr. Anya Krishnan / O1-241' case is the visual hero of the dashboard, below the real cases
expected: As a paralegal with 20-30 real matters, my dashboard should foreground MY cases, not a hardcoded sample one with its own Open/Voice-intake buttons.
got: Below YourCasesCard, the dashboard always renders the mock masthead (O1-241, Krishnan), CriteriaTable, TasksCard, PetitionDraftCard, and CaseList from lib/data/cases.ts. This is the accepted illustrative demo portfolio, but it competes visually with the real list and its 'Open petition letter' button is non-functional. Noted as scope, not a defect (accepted-gaps: mock demo case file).
suggested_acceptance: Out of scope per accepted-gaps (mock demo portfolio). If revisited, collapse/hide the demo masthead once the user has real cases.
l2_priority: n-a

title: Qualify is grounded on the real pasted profile, returns the full canonical 8 criteria, honours the >=3 threshold, never renders None green
expected: Verdict reflects what I pasted; 8 CFR 214.2(o)(3)(iii) criteria represented; >=3 threshold honoured; unscored criteria never green; DISCLAIMER present.
got: buildQualifyPrompt embeds the real profile text + the pack's exact criterion names with an explicit 'don't let one criterion satisfy another' rule. parseQualifyResponse always returns the pack's full canonical criteria set in order, filling omissions with 'None'; coerceStatus defaults unknown to 'None'. summarizeCriteria counts only Met/Strong toward the threshold and statusTone renders None neutral. O-1A pack threshold = 3. DISCLAIMER renders first and non-dismissible; a live qualify adjudication gate checks canonical criteria/valid statuses/likelihood range/UPL tripwire.
suggested_acceptance: Keep the canonical-fill + None-never-green + threshold-from-pack invariants.
l2_priority: Live: confirm the verdict names the lead's REAL evidence (not placeholders) and that scoring is calibrated, not inflated.

title: Citation discipline is enforced end-to-end: exhibits bound from the real vault, inline (Exhibit N) audited, hallucinated cites quarantined, fabricated specifics scanned live
expected: Drafts cite only supplied evidence; zero fabricated exhibits/citations; DISCLAIMER on every AI payload; a mock is never billed as model output.
got: attachExhibits binds (Exhibit N) only to real vault documents (route-side, never from the inline body); the prompt forbids inventing exhibits and citing case law; auditCitations surfaces unresolved (hallucinated) citation numbers and a coverage meter; the live adjudication gate scans output for specifics not traceable to the criteria text (shared single-source-of-truth with the eval harness), case-law cites, wrong visa codes, and disclaimer presence; the orchestrator reclaims the charge and labels source 'mock' when JSON is unusable, so boilerplate is never billed as model output. DISCLAIMER rides on every AI build envelope (wrapResult / buildXResult).
suggested_acceptance: This is the spine that earns a burned-by-AI paralegal's trust — do not weaken the exhibit binding, the citation audit, or the live fabrication gate.
l2_priority: Live: feed real evidence, assert the draft cites the supplied exhibits by number, names no exhibit not on file, and the adjudication badge reflects a clean run.

title: FAQ contradicts the site's "drafting tool, not a law firm" positioning — reads as a flat-fee firm-of-record
expected: The FAQ — the surface a careful attorney-buyer uses to kill her UPL objection — should reinforce the same positioning as the rest of the site: a self-serve, token-metered AI drafting tool whose output is work product reviewed and signed by the user's OWN attorney of record; not legal advice; priced in prepaid tokens.
got: The FAQ describes a full-service immigration law firm on a flat-fee retainer: "the same attorney listed as counsel of record" reads the petition (q2), "we coordinate the Application Support Center appointment" for biometrics (q5), RFE response "is part of the flat fee" (q3), "the attorney portion of the flat fee is non-refundable" (q7), and "your attorney of record answers directly" to emailed questions (intro). This directly contradicts the landing page's "We're a drafting tool, not a law firm" (page.tsx:254-255) and "your OWN attorney of record" framing — blurring exactly the UPL/who-is-liable line the rest of the site draws carefully.
suggested_acceptance: Rewrite the FAQ entries so they describe the actual product: the platform drafts work product, the USER brings/owns their attorney of record (the platform does not supply counsel), pricing is prepaid tokens (not a flat legal fee), and refund language refers to token purchases (per billing/page.tsx footnotes), not an 'attorney portion of the flat fee'. The 'not a law firm / not legal advice' line must be consistent with landing + billing + validation.
l2_priority: Confirm live the FAQ copy as rendered, and whether any other surface (welcome/onboarding/consent) repeats the firm-of-record framing.

title: The /validation evidence page is unreachable from any cold-prospect marketing surface
expected: The validation/correctness page — the strongest credibility artifact for an attorney-buyer (acceptance criterion 5) — should be discoverable from the cold-entry path (landing/billing/faq nav or footer) so a prospect evaluating in ~90 seconds can actually find it.
got: No marketing surface links to /validation. The landing footer links How-it-works, /billing, /faq, /landing-claude but not /validation (no /validation href in src/app/page.tsx). The only link to /validation in the codebase is inside the AUTHENTICATED /qualify panel (QualifyPanel.tsx:165), downstream of the adoption decision. A cold prospect would never reach the one page that most moves her verdict.
suggested_acceptance: Add a /validation link to the shared marketing footer (and ideally the FAQ 'data security / correctness' answer), so the evidence page is one click from a cold landing. The gating itself — not the page — is the finding; the page content is a strength.
l2_priority: Confirm live nav/footer reachability of /validation from /, /billing, /faq without authentication.

title: FAQ names "Gemini" as the drafter; engine attribution is inconsistent / dated
expected: Public-facing copy should either name the engine consistently or stay engine-agnostic; a careful buyer notices a specific vendor name and checks whether it matches the rest of the story.
got: FAQ q2 says "Once Gemini drafts your petition letter..."; the qualify route comment and env describe a multi-engine wrapper (Gemini|Claude|mock) with the local UAT engine set to Claude. A named single vendor in marketing copy reads as stale/inconsistent to a skeptical reader and undercuts the otherwise-careful positioning.
suggested_acceptance: Make the FAQ engine-agnostic (e.g. 'our AI drafts your petition letter') or align the named engine with the product's actual configuration.
l2_priority: n-a (static copy).

title: /landing-claude alt masthead has no FAQ/validation/pricing link and only an inline (not stamped) disclaimer
expected: An alternate landing a prospect might arrive on cold should still route her to the objection-killing surfaces (FAQ, validation, pricing) and carry the not-legal-advice line clearly.
got: /landing-claude links only to /qualify and /dashboard; its footer is 'Immigration Concierge · AI drafting for O-1 / EB-1 · 2026' with no FAQ/pricing/validation links. The UPL line appears only inline in body copy ('never legal advice', lines 63/154), not as the prominent DisclaimerStamp block used elsewhere. A prospect entering here has fewer paths to resolve compliance/pricing objections.
suggested_acceptance: If /landing-claude is a live entry point, add footer links to FAQ/billing/validation. (If it is an internal A/B/demo masthead not used cold, downgrade to scope_note.) The inline 'never legal advice' is acceptable for a marketing page that surfaces no AI output.
l2_priority: Confirm whether /landing-claude is a real cold-entry route or an internal demo; check footer/nav reachability.

title: Free hero verdict is keyword/regex-only and can under-score a strong record phrased off-keyword
expected: The free screener should return a verdict at least as sharp as an attorney's back-of-envelope read; a strong candidate should not be mis-scored merely for phrasing achievements without trigger words.
got: The anonymous hero preview runs mockQualification — regex keyword matches per criterion (packs.ts:38-94) — not the model. A criterion is 'Met' only on a literal keyword hit, so e.g. chairing a standards committee without the words judge/review/panel scores 'None'. It honestly reflects the pasted text and is NOT a husk (it names evidence, honors 3-of-8, never greens 'None'), and the SoftGate clearly frames it as the instant read with the real model behind /qualify — so this is by-design and disclosed, not a defect, but it caps the free read's senior-quality ceiling.
suggested_acceptance: Keep the keyless preview as the floor (cost/abuse-safe is correct). Ensure the SoftGate copy makes the keyword-floor nature legible (it largely does). No structural change required.
l2_priority: On authenticated /qualify, fill a real strong-candidate profile and assert the LIVE model output names the supplied evidence and crosswalks the correct criterion (i.e. the model path is meaningfully sharper than the keyword floor).

title: Pricing + free grant sourced from canonical economy.ts BUNDLES — cannot drift between landing and billing
expected: Bundle prices and the free grant should be one source of truth so marketing and billing can never disagree.
got: Both / and /billing import BUNDLES and FREE_SIGNUP_GRANT from src/lib/tokens/economy.ts; the landing BundleCard is explicitly 'Driven from economy.ts BUNDLES so the price/tokens can't drift'. Enterprise contact-wall is correctly scoped to the enterprise tier only — the core offer is fully self-serve.
suggested_acceptance: Protect: keep all price/grant rendering sourced from economy.ts; do not hardcode prices in copy.
l2_priority: n-a

title: /validation is real evidence — primary-source citations, verified-vs-counsel-approved two-layer honesty, CI-gated
expected: A 'validation' page should show evidence, not adjectives.
got: PROGRAM_VALIDATIONS / COMPLIANCE_VALIDATIONS carry actual primary-law and agency-guidance citations (eCFR, Cornell LII, USCIS Policy Manual, Arizona Supreme Court ABS order PDF), legal basis (8 CFR 214.2(o)(3)(iii) etc.), thresholds, review dates, a freshness countdown, and an explicit two-layer model separating 'verified (matches sources)' from 'counsel-approved (cleared to file)'. UK is self-flagged 'needs-review' with a MODEL MISMATCH note. validation.test.ts CI-gates that no live program ships unverified.
suggested_acceptance: Protect, and surface it from the marketing footer (see kw-eval-02). This is the single strongest credibility artifact for an attorney-buyer.
l2_priority: n-a

title: DISCLAIMER rides first and non-dismissibly on every screening; mock + model both grounded in real pasted input
expected: The not-legal-advice line must accompany every AI output, and the verdict must reflect what the user actually pasted.
got: One DISCLAIMER constant (lib/result.ts:37) is attached in one factory (buildQualifyResult) and rendered FIRST as a non-aria-hidden role='note' DisclaimerStamp by the shared CriteriaReport. The keyless mock scores against the user's real text and names matched evidence; the model prompt injects req.profile and forbids fabrication. 'None' never renders green and never counts toward the 3-of-8 threshold.
suggested_acceptance: Protect: keep the single-disclaimer-constant + single-factory invariant; do not let any AI route render output without DisclaimerStamp.
l2_priority: n-a

title: Share certificate carries only postable facts (never profile text), is tamper-checked, and framed as informational not a legal grant
expected: A shareable verdict must not leak private data and must not overclaim a legal determination.
got: encodeSnapshot encodes only name/classification/likelihood/per-criterion status chars — never the profile text (explicit comment). /c/[token] and its OG card decode from the token alone (no DB) and render from the real criteria pack; decodeSnapshot returns null (->404) for non-live programs or mismatched criteria counts. Page framing is 'Informational only · not legal advice · no account needed' and the stamp reads 'Qualifies / In progress' against the pack threshold, not a legal grant.
suggested_acceptance: Protect: keep the token encoding minimal (no profile text) and the framing informational.
l2_priority: n-a

title: With ATTORNEY_EMAILS empty, developer@localhost is denied the entire attorney workflow — contradicting the journey/fixture/accepted-gaps demo-unlock premise
expected: Per env.md fixture + accepted-gaps + the journey reachability note: empty ATTORNEY_EMAILS = demo-unlock, so developer@localhost reaches the review queue and can request-changes / sign & file / record decision.
got: Every attorney gate now uses the STRICT isConfiguredAttorney, which fails CLOSED on an empty allowlist (roles.ts:45 returns false). Result for developer@localhost with ATTORNEY_EMAILS unset: no 'Review queue' nav link (DashboardView.tsx:35 gated on dashboard/page.tsx:25), the queue page renders 'Your account isn't on the attorney allowlist' (review/page.tsx:17 -> ReviewQueueView.tsx:71-79), ReviewPanel renders zero attorney action blocks (CaseDetailView passes attorney=isConfiguredAttorney, ReviewPanel.tsx:125/149 hidden), and even a forged POST is rejected by requireAttorney (actions.ts:51). The journey is unreachable as the fixtures instruct.
suggested_acceptance: Either (a) update env.md/accepted-gaps/journey to instruct setting ATTORNEY_EMAILS=developer@localhost as the demo fixture (the code is correctly fail-closed and the README/comments already say so), or (b) add a dev-only allowlist default. Without one of these the J1/J5 attorney journeys cannot be exercised by the documented dev user.
l2_priority: Set ATTORNEY_EMAILS=developer@localhost, then verify the whole round-trip lives: queue lists oldest-first with truthful age badges, request-changes returns to Drafting with the note visible in the thread, sign & file advances to Filed + mints a receipt, record decision -> Approved.

title: 'Sign & file with USCIS' is a bare one-click submit — no confirmation, no statement of effect, for the single most consequential action under the attorney's bar license
expected: The action that signs the petition under counsel's name and files it with USCIS should make its effect clear and intentional — a confirmation step or an explicit 'this signs and files; the case advances to Filed and gets a receipt number' so it is never a surprise click.
got: The button is a plain form submit bound directly to attorneySignAndFile (ReviewPanel.tsx:130-134) with no confirm dialog, no effect description, and no irreversibility/intentionality note (grep for confirm/irreversible in the panel finds none). The adjacent applicant 'Submit for review' has explanatory microprint; the far more consequential attorney action has none.
suggested_acceptance: Add a confirmation affordance (a confirm step or an inline 'This signs the petition and files it with USCIS — the case moves to Filed and receives a receipt number') before attorneySignAndFile runs.
l2_priority: Confirm live there is no interstitial; judge whether the one-click filing reads as intentional to an attorney whose signature is on the line.

title: The RFE response route does NOT run the live adjudication gate that the draft route runs — fabrication/case-law/wrong-code signals are surfaced on drafts but not on RFE responses
expected: Parity: the same runtime fabrication / case-law-flag / wrong-classification adjudication report that rides the draft response (draftOperation.ts:167 -> runAdjudication) should also ride the RFE response the attorney signs, since the RFE is an equally signable USCIS filing.
got: The RFE spec (api/rfe/route.ts:45-140) defines parse/prompt/guard/mock/build/persist but NO adjudicate hook, so executeAiOperation attaches no adjudication report to the RFE body (operation.ts:362-369 runs only when spec.adjudicate exists). The citation-discipline PROMPT is present and equivalent, but the runtime safety net that catches a hallucinated cite for the attorney is absent on this surface.
suggested_acceptance: Add an `adjudicate` hook to the RFE spec wiring runAdjudication({ operation: 'rfe', ... }) — the gate already has an 'rfe' branch (adjudication-gates.ts:299), so it is a few lines for parity.
l2_priority: Confirm live the RFE response body lacks an adjudication report while the draft has one; assess whether the missing runtime fabrication flag matters once the prompt discipline holds on the grounded path.

title: Citation discipline + UPL line are load-bearing and enforced at three layers — prompt, envelope, and a live adjudication gate
expected: An attorney needs the tool to never fabricate authority and to never present output as legal advice.
got: Prompt forbids inventing awards/publications/exhibits and bars case-law ('the attorney of record will add any case-law authorities') with prompt-injection data markers (drafting.ts:181-208). The single DISCLAIMER ('not legal advice... an attorney of record... is required to review... before anything is filed') is attached via the one wrapResult chokepoint and rides every success AND error body (result.ts:37, operation.ts:255/278/341). A live adjudication gate scores each paid draft for fabricated specifics, leaked visa codes, and case-law cites and surfaces them to the attorney (adjudication-gates.ts:294, draftOperation.ts:167). DB path feeds REAL persisted criteria + REAL vault exhibits, not sample data (draftOperation.ts:106-121).
suggested_acceptance: Protect this — do not weaken the DISCLAIMER string, the chokepoint, or the adjudication gate.
l2_priority: On the grounded path, confirm the live draft actually NAMES the supplied evidence/exhibits and crosswalks the correct criterion (real prose quality), and that the adjudication report reads honestly.

title: Inline (Exhibit N) citations are audited against on-file exhibits — a hallucinated exhibit number is quarantined as 'unresolved'
expected: The attorney must never sign a letter that cites evidence the case doesn't have.
got: auditCitations/auditDraftCitations (drafting.ts:556-580) parse every (Exhibit N) token and split into resolved vs unresolved against the real vault exhibit ordinals; unresolved is the 'you can never ship a letter that cites evidence you don't have' safety signal, and exhibit numbers are bound to real StoredDocuments by the route (attachExhibits), never to untrusted inline text.
suggested_acceptance: Keep the unresolved-citation quarantine; surface it prominently in the UI so the attorney sees it during review.
l2_priority: Confirm the unresolved-citation count is visibly surfaced to the attorney in the DraftStudio/ExhibitIndex during review, not just computed.

title: The status lifecycle and roadmap stepper derive correctly from real case state
expected: Status must be legible and accurate at every step (Intake/Drafting -> Review -> Filed -> Decision) and the next action obvious.
got: caseRoadmap (roadmap.ts:38-59) derives done/current/upcoming from the real status plus hasEvidence/hasDraft; CaseDetailView feeds it from real documents/draft state (CaseDetailView.tsx:133-137). Transitions are an atomic compare-and-set (pglite-store.ts:480-522) so a double-clicked sign&file can't double-file or mint a second receipt, and the append-only review thread can't desync from status. 'Approved' is correctly terminal (actions.ts:184-190).
suggested_acceptance: Protect the CAS transition and the state-derived stepper.
l2_priority: Walk a real case through Drafting -> Review -> Filed -> Approved and confirm the stepper + status badges update correctly at each step.

title: Queue is honestly oldest-first with truthful age badges, and the cross-tenant queue read fails closed
expected: The queue must not hide an overdue case; oldest-first; truthful fresh/warning/overdue badges; no cross-tenant data leak.
got: getCasesInReview orders by created_at asc at the DB (pglite-store.ts:433-441) AND the view re-sorts oldest-first (ReviewQueueView.tsx:44 via sortOldestFirst). ageBucket is truthful (fresh <12h, warning 12-24h, overdue >24h) and null-safe for future timestamps (queue-age.ts:26-38); the badge carries an aria-label. The queue is a cross-tenant read gated by isConfiguredAttorney (fail-closed) so it can't be used to enumerate other applicants' cases/IDs (review/page.tsx:8-17).
suggested_acceptance: Protect the oldest-first sort, the age-bucket thresholds, and the fail-closed cross-tenant gate.
l2_priority: With ATTORNEY_EMAILS set, confirm a >24h case shows the overdue (danger) badge and sorts to the top.

title: FAQ describes a full-service law firm, contradicting the site-wide 'drafting tool, not a law firm / your own attorney signs' positioning
expected: Positioning consistent across landing/FAQ/pricing: a self-serve drafting tool whose output is work product for the USER'S OWN attorney of record to review and sign; not legal advice.
got: The FAQ presents a managed legal service: 'a licensed U.S. immigration attorney - the same attorney listed as counsel of record - reads the petition line by line ... and signs the I-129 themselves'; flat fees; 'pre-drafted RFE responses are included on every ... packet'; biometrics scheduling; premium-processing pass-through; an ATA translator bench; 'we comply with attorney-client privilege.' This is the opposite of the landing/billing model (your own attorney signs; we are not a law firm).
suggested_acceptance: Rewrite the FAQ so it describes the actual product: the AI drafts work product; the user's own attorney of record (not a firm attorney supplied by the product) reviews, edits, and signs; tokens (not flat fees) are the price model; RFE responses are a drafting feature, not an included service; remove biometrics/translator/privilege claims that imply a law firm. Add a CI/lint check or shared positioning copy so FAQ can't drift from the landing's UPL line.
l2_priority: Confirm live that the FAQ copy is reachable from the prospect funnel and still contradicts the landing; check /landing-claude for the same drift.

title: Draft is grounded on qualify-time per-criterion paraphrases + vault exhibit facts, not the full pasted CV
expected: The draft prompt should be able to argue from the petitioner's full supplied record (her rich pasted CV), not just short paraphrases.
got: The DB draft path loads each criterion's persisted evidence/rationale (the model's SHORT paraphrase captured at screening) and fuses in evidence-vault exhibit facts; it never re-reads the original pasted profile text. A rich CV pasted at qualify but not re-entered into the Evidence Vault yields a thin per-criterion grounding payload. Machinery is correct and anti-fabrication; grounding depth is bounded by what's persisted/vaulted.
suggested_acceptance: Either prompt the user to populate the Evidence Vault before drafting (so exhibit facts enrich the prompt), or persist/optionally pass the original profile text as additional grounding context, so the draft can argue from her full record rather than a one-line paraphrase per criterion.
l2_priority: Fill a real rich CV + a populated vault and assert the live draft NAMES her specific evidence and cites the right exhibits per section - vs. generic filler when the vault is empty.

title: /billing copy 'one token = one AI form-field guidance answer' understates the real per-operation cost
expected: The cost story a careful prospect reads should match the real metering (qualify=3, draft=12, section/rfe=5, categorize=1).
got: /billing repeatedly frames a token as 'one AI form-field guidance answer' / 'each AI form-field answer costs a single token', legacy copy from the old form-field product. The real registry meters a full draft at 12 tokens. The LANDING footnote already self-corrects ('a full petition draft spends more than a single form-field answer'), so the inconsistency is internal to /billing.
suggested_acceptance: Align /billing's 'what a token buys' copy with the registry (show per-op costs, e.g. draft = 12 tokens), matching the landing footnote.
l2_priority: Low - copy alignment; verify the per-op cost chips render on the live billing/qualify/draft surfaces.

title: Categorization sees one document at a time, with no cross-vault context
expected: Filing-quality organization might consider documents together (de-dupe, group related exhibits, avoid splitting one piece of evidence across buckets).
got: buildCategorizePrompt is fed only the single document's name + text; it cannot see other already-filed documents. Exhibit-number monotonicity is handled correctly at the store, and refile is available to correct a mis-bucket, but the model has no whole-vault view.
suggested_acceptance: Acceptable for MVP. If hardened: pass a compact summary of existing buckets so the model can place a doc consistently with siblings.
l2_priority: Categorize several related docs live and check each lands in the correct one of the eight buckets with accurate extracted facts and a sane exhibit index.

title: Anonymous hero verdict + best-path run the keyword mock, not the model (labelled, by design)
expected: A 'free read' that reflects what she pasted with adjudicator-grade reasoning.
got: /api/qualify/preview and .../best-path deliberately run only the deterministic keyword mock (no model, no charge, no DB) for anonymous traffic; results are labelled source:'mock' and 'instant read', and the SoftGate invites the real screening behind sign-in. The real, model-backed scoring is the authenticated /api/qualify path she reaches after sign-in. Honest and clearly labelled - recorded so L2 confirms she lands on the model path, not so it is treated as a defect.
suggested_acceptance: None needed (by design + labelled). Optionally make the hero's 'instant read vs full screening' distinction even more explicit so a rigorous user doesn't mistake the keyword read for the model verdict.
l2_priority: Confirm the authenticated /qualify screening returns source:'claude' (real model) and names her pasted evidence, distinct from the hero's mock read.

title: 'Certificate of Extraordinary Ability' / 'Approved' framing skirts her horoscope pet peeve
expected: An evidence-minded user wants a sober, defensible read - not award-ceremony theater.
got: The hero renders the screening as an engraved 'Certificate of Extraordinary Ability' with a Seal/Guilloche, and the static hero card shows a sample 'Status: Filing-ready / Stamp: Approved / 92% likelihood'. The underlying content is her real scored criteria + honest gaps and the live read is labelled 'free informational read, never legal advice', so it is styling over honest data, not a fabricated verdict.
suggested_acceptance: Optional: tone the 'Approved/Certificate' branding toward 'informational screening' for high-rigor segments; keep the honest data underneath untouched.
l2_priority: Subjective - judge live whether the certificate theater undermines or aids credibility for a researcher persona.

title: Unmet ('None') criteria never render green; tone and threshold counts are single-sourced
expected: Nothing she didn't claim shows as 'Met'; unscored criteria never render green.
got: classifyStatus maps 'None'/unknown to 'other' -> neutral tone, excluded from the qualifying count; statusTone and summarizeCriteria both derive from it so badge and count can't disagree (ADR-0002). Met/Strong count toward threshold, Partial does not.
suggested_acceptance: Protect: do not let any surface paint a row green off an unscored status.
l2_priority: Verify live a 'None' criterion renders neutral and is excluded from the qualifying count.

title: Real case + real vault exhibits fed in <<<CASE_DATA>>>; hallucinated exhibit citations are quarantined
expected: Zero fabricated awards/citations/exhibits; each section crosswalks to the correct criterion.
got: buildDraftPrompt embeds the real persisted criteria + fused vault exhibits inside <<<CASE_DATA>>> markers with hard anti-fabrication + injection-defense rules and a listed-exhibit-only citation rule; auditCitations flags any (Exhibit N) with no matching on-file document as 'unresolved'; the live adjudication gate runs the fabricated-specifics scan and case-law flag on every draft.
suggested_acceptance: Protect: keep the CASE_DATA fencing, citation-only-from-listed rule, and the unresolved-citation quarantine.
l2_priority: Confirm a live draft cites only real exhibits and flags any hallucinated (Exhibit N).

title: /validation shows primary-source evidence and self-flags its own paraphrasing + the UK model mismatch
expected: The validation page shows evidence, not adjectives; criteria represented honestly.
got: Each program/compliance claim carries a legal basis (8 CFR 214.2(o)(3)(iii), 204.5(h)(3)), primary-law + USCIS Policy Manual source links, review date + 180-day freshness. Notes openly state O-1A/O-1B labels are 'paraphrased ... verbatim wording + counsel sign-off pending' and flag the UK pack as a 'MODEL MISMATCH ... the model is wrong for the UK' (correctly gated planned). This honesty directly answers her 'criteria summarized wrong' worry.
suggested_acceptance: Protect: keep the cited-source + freshness + self-disclosed-paraphrase model; it is a major trust asset for rigorous users.
l2_priority: Verify the live source links resolve and the freshness/last-reviewed dates render.

title: Landing-hero verdict and best-path run the keyword mock, not the model; a pure GitHub/product founder is under-scored on the free read
expected: The first verdict Sam sees reflects his real founder evidence (GitHub/product => original contribution) believably, or is clearly labelled as a lighter teaser.
got: The hero InstantVerdict and the best-path finder call /api/qualify/preview and /api/qualify/preview/best-path, which run ONLY mockQualification (regex keyword match), never the model, by design (no signup/charge). The O-1A ORIGINAL and CRITICAL_ROLE regexes match patent/founder/CEO but NOT GitHub-stars / open-source / 'shipped product', so a founder whose strength is product/GitHub (no press, no patent) gets a low keyword read until the authenticated MODEL screening runs. The 'go deeper' CTA copy ('This was the instant read') hints at the distinction but does not say the engine itself is weaker.
suggested_acceptance: Either widen the keyless ORIGINAL/CRITICAL_ROLE heuristics to catch GitHub/open-source/product-launch signals, or add one line on the hero/soft-gate making explicit that the instant read is a keyword preview and the full model screening (behind sign-in) reads the nuance.
l2_priority: Confirm the AUTHENTICATED model screening maps founder GitHub/product evidence to 'Original contribution' (and round/ARR to 'High remuneration', press to 'Press') correctly on Sam's real pasted profile.

title: Authenticated screening feeds the real pasted profile to the prompt; disclaimer is first and non-dismissible; unscored criteria can never render green
expected: A believable verdict grounded in what Sam actually pasted, with the UPL line load-bearing and the criteria math honest.
got: buildQualifyPrompt injects req.name + req.profile verbatim and forbids inventing facts; DISCLAIMER is attached to every result and rendered first/non-dismissible; statusTone/summarizeCriteria are single-sourced so a 'None' criterion is neutral, never green, and the count can never disagree with the rows.
suggested_acceptance: Keep the single-sourced criteria classifier and the prompt's real-profile grounding intact under any refactor.
l2_priority: Verify the live verdict NAMES Sam's real evidence (not placeholders) and that likelihood/criteria read believably.

title: Live fabrication tripwire + citation audit directly defend Sam's 'no fabricated stars/ARR/press' bar
expected: The draft must not invent metrics, and any cited evidence must trace to something real.
got: The DB-path draft is grounded in the case's persisted criteria + vault exhibits (attachExhibits). The prompt wraps applicant data in <<<CASE_DATA>>> with an injection guard and forbids inventing awards/citations/numbers and case law. runAdjudication runs on every paid draft: fabricatedSpecifics flags $/%/years/big-ints (citation/star counts) in the output that aren't in the input, surfaced as a visible 'verify:' AdjudicationBadge; auditCitations quarantines any (Exhibit N) that doesn't resolve to an on-file document.
suggested_acceptance: Preserve the fabrication/case-law/wrong-code gates and the citation audit on the paid path; keep the badge visible to the user.
l2_priority: On a real grounded draft, confirm the badge actually fires when prose introduces an unsupported metric, and that legitimate supplied numbers do NOT false-positive.

title: Keyless mock-draft argues from templated evidence strings, not Sam's words (keyless build only; L2 runs the real model)
expected: Each section argues from Sam's actual supplied facts.
got: On the keyless build (no engine), mockQualification sets evidence to a template ('Mentions an award, prize, or honor.') and mockDraft argues from those templates, so a keyless draft reads generic. This is the deterministic fallback, clearly labelled 'Placeholder output' in the UI, and does NOT apply to the L2 env (LLM_ENGINE=claude). Flagged only so the keyless path isn't mistaken for the product's real output.
suggested_acceptance: No change required; the 'Placeholder output' banner already flags it. Out of scope per the keyless/mock boundary.
l2_priority: The whole ballgame: assert the LIVE Claude draft prose names Sam's real evidence, crosswalks the right criterion, and reads like a senior immigration drafter (not filler) on the grounded path.

title: Billing page headline 'one token = one AI form-field guidance answer' undersells a 12-token full draft
expected: Sam can see what a draft costs before committing, with no mismatch between the billing framing and the real per-op cost.
got: /billing says 'Each AI form-field answer costs a single token' and the footnote repeats '1 token = one AI form-field guidance answer' (legacy guidance framing), but a qualify costs 3 and a full draft 12. The mismatch is mitigated: the landing pricing footnote already corrects it ('a full petition draft spends more than a single form-field answer'), and per-op costs are shown at point-of-use (QualifyPanel '{costOf(qualify)} tokens', DraftStudio 'Uses 12 tokens'). So acceptance #5 (see cost before committing) holds; this is a clarity nit on the billing page, not a dark pattern.
suggested_acceptance: Update the /billing headline + footnote to reflect that different AI operations cost different amounts (qualify 3, draft 12, section/RFE 5), e.g. a small per-op price list driven from the OperationRegistry.

title: Letters Patent is a stateless, privacy-safe, tamper-rejecting certificate with a per-result OG card
expected: A credible share link Sam would actually post, with nothing private leaking and honest framing.
got: The token encodes only name/classification/likelihood/per-criterion status (never the profile text). /c/[token] renders from the token alone (no DB); decode rejects a non-live program or a status count that doesn't match the pack, so a tampered token can't render a bogus coat-of-arms. A per-result opengraph-image unfurls on LinkedIn/X and degrades gracefully. Framing: 'Informational only - not legal advice - no account needed'.
suggested_acceptance: Keep the snapshot minimal (no profile text) and the decode validation strict.
l2_priority: Visually confirm the /c/[token] page and OG card render credibly (not cheesy) and the LinkedIn unfurl looks like a real certificate.

title: Dashboard suppresses the real-case list when TOKENS_BYPASS=1 (the mode env.md recommends for the quality journeys)
expected: From /dashboard Sam sees his real cases above the mock demo and opens one to track progress.
got: DashboardPage short-circuits when TOKENS_BYPASS=1 OR no store, leaving cases=[] (it skips getCasesForUser), so only the mock portfolio shows and YourCasesCard never lists the real case. The case IS still created (qualify's persist runs because getUser() returns DEV_USER even under bypass) and is reachable via the /qualify -> 'Open case file' deep-link (CaseDetailPage uses requireOnboardedUser unconditionally). In real-economy mode (bypass off) the list works correctly. So J5's 'dashboard lists my real cases' holds without bypass and breaks only with bypass on.
suggested_acceptance: Decouple the dashboard case-list load from the metering bypass: list real cases whenever a user resolves (dev-auth or Firebase), gating only the BALANCE pill on the economy being enforced.
l2_priority: Run J5 in BOTH modes: confirm real cases list on /dashboard in real-economy mode, and confirm whether they list (or not) under TOKENS_BYPASS=1; decide if the bypass suppression is acceptable for the demo.
